chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Appl. No. S- 1078 of 2025 `DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 09.02.2026 For orders on office objections For hearing of main case Mr. Wajid Ali Khaskheli, Advocate for applicants Mr. Altaf Hussain Khokhar, A.P.G. O R D E R Omar Sial, J. - Raza Muhammad and Fayaz seek post-arrest bail in crime number 101 of 2023, registered under sections 302, 324, 337-A(i), 337-F(i), 114, 504, and 35 P.P.C. at the Kadhan police station. This is the second bail application filed by the two applicants. The first application or its order has not been attached, but the applicant’s learned counsel admits that Criminal Bail Application No. 665 of 2024 was filed by the same applicants in the High Court and was dismissed. In response to a query from counsel about what new ground had emerged, he said that three witnesses had been examined at trial and that, if one reviews their testimonies, it will be seen that the applicants should be granted bail. 2. I most respectfully disagree with the submission made by the counsel. A review and analysis of the testimonies of three witnesses will lead to a deeper appreciation of the evidence, which is not envisaged at the bail stage. There are still prosecution witnesses to be examined, and the learned trial court's report shows that the applicants themselves, by filing transfer applications, are delaying the conclusion of the trial. Any observation or findings made by the High Court at this stage could inadvertently impact the judgment of the learned trial court. 3. The bail application is dismissed. The learned trial court should proceed with the trial and should not be hampered by the mere filing of a transfer application.

The bail application is dismissed. The learned trial court should proceed with the trial and should not be hampered by the mere filing of a transfer application. The learned Assistant Prosecutor General submits that the transfer application may also have been dismissed. Let a copy of this order be sent to the learned trial court. JUDGE karar_hussain/PS*
